title: Production hardening — retry policy, step timeout, token estimation, compaction trigger, variable expansion
purpose: Five production-grade gaps identified during the thoroughness audit. Without these, the runtime works but lacks the resilience and polish needed for real deployment: provider calls don't retry on transient failures, there's no overall step timeout, no client-side token estimation for context window planning, no automatic compaction trigger when context grows large, and session variable {{var}} expansion isn't integrated into system instructions or tool descriptions.
task: Implement provider retry policy with configurable backoff for transient errors. Add step-level timeout wrapping the entire run_agent_step call. Add client-side token estimation using tiktoken-compatible counting for context window planning. Add automatic compaction trigger when token usage crosses a threshold. Wire session variable expand() into system instruction and tool description rendering. Testing: unit tests for each feature plus integration tests for retry and timeout.

requirements.title: Provider retry policy for transient failures
requirements.spec: When a provider call fails with a transient error (network timeout, 502/503/504, connection reset), the loop SHALL retry with configurable backoff instead of immediately failing.
acceptance: RetryPolicy struct: max_retries (u32, default 2), initial_backoff (Duration, default 1s), backoff_multiplier (f64, default 2.0), retryable_errors (Vec<RetryableError>)
RetryableError enum: NetworkTimeout, ConnectionReset, ServerError { status: u16 }, RateLimited (already handled by rate limiter but included for completeness)
retry_with_backoff(policy, provider_call) -> Result<AssembledResponse, ProviderError>
Each retry logs at warn level with attempt number and error
Non-retryable errors (400, 401, 422) fail immediately without retry
Total retry duration capped at max_retries * max_backoff to prevent unbounded waits
LoopContext gains field: retry_policy (Option<RetryPolicy>)
Test: mock provider fails twice with 503 then succeeds, verify 3 attempts made with backoff

requirements.title: Step-level timeout
requirements.spec: run_agent_step SHALL support an overall timeout that kills the entire step if it exceeds a configurable duration, regardless of what the loop is doing.
acceptance: AgentLoopConfig gains field: step_timeout (Option<Duration>)
When set, run_agent_step wraps its main loop in tokio::time::timeout
Timeout produces AgentStepResult::TimedOut { elapsed: Duration, iterations: usize, partial_output: Option<Value> }
TimedOut variant added to AgentStepResult enum
Partial output captures whatever the model produced before timeout (last assistant text, if any)
Test: mock provider that sleeps 5s, step_timeout 100ms, verify TimedOut returned

requirements.title: Client-side token estimation
requirements.spec: Before sending a provider request, the loop SHALL estimate the token count of the prompt to detect context window overflow before it happens.
acceptance: TokenEstimator trait: fn estimate(&self, text: &str) -> usize
SimpleTokenEstimator: chars / 4 as a rough approximation (no external dependency)
LoopContext gains field: token_estimator (Option<Box<dyn TokenEstimator>>)
Before call_provider, estimate total prompt tokens from messages
If estimated tokens exceed a configurable context_window_limit (from AgentLoopConfig), emit a TokenWarning event and optionally trigger compaction
Estimation is advisory — does not block the call (the provider returns the actual error if context overflows)
Test: set context_window_limit to 100 tokens, send a 200-token prompt, verify TokenWarning emitted

requirements.title: Automatic compaction trigger
requirements.spec: When token usage crosses a configurable threshold, the loop SHALL automatically trigger context compaction using the session's ContextEdits.
acceptance: AgentLoopConfig gains field: auto_compact_threshold_pct (Option<f64>, e.g. 0.75 for 75% of context window)
When estimated tokens > threshold * context_window_limit, trigger auto-compaction
Auto-compaction: suppress all events before the most recent N turns (configurable, default 10), append a Compaction event summarizing suppressed content
Compaction summary is a structured JSON with: event_count_suppressed, token_estimate_freed, turn_range
Compaction only fires once per threshold crossing (not every iteration while over threshold)
Test: fill session with events exceeding threshold, verify Compaction event appended and older events suppressed

requirements.title: Wire variable expansion into system instructions and tool descriptions
requirements.spec: Session variable {{var_name}} patterns SHALL be expanded in system instructions and tool descriptions before they reach the provider. The expand() function from N-016 exists but is not called anywhere in the loop.
acceptance: LoopContext gains field: variables (Option<VariableStore>)
Before call_provider, expand {{var}} patterns in the system instruction sections
Before call_provider, expand {{var}} patterns in tool descriptions (ToolDefinition.description field)
Expansion failures (unknown variable, shell command timeout) produce diagnostic warnings but do not block the call — unexpanded {{var}} patterns pass through unchanged
Built-in variables (session_id, working_dir, home_dir) available without explicit registration
Test: set variable 'project' to 'norn', system instruction contains '{{project}}', verify expanded to 'norn' in provider request

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement tiktoken or cl100k_base tokenizer — SimpleTokenEstimator (chars/4) is sufficient for v1
SHALL NOT implement LLM-based compaction summaries — auto-compaction uses structured metadata, not model summarization
SHALL NOT implement retry for non-transient errors (auth failures, invalid requests)
SHALL NOT implement circuit-breaker patterns — retry policy is per-call, not per-provider
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all tests pass
Provider retry retries on 503 and succeeds on third attempt — verified by test
Step timeout returns TimedOut — verified by test
Token estimation detects context window overflow — verified by test
Auto-compaction fires at threshold — verified by test
Variable expansion replaces {{var}} in system instruction — verified by test
